problem_solving_patterns.description: Consistently designs module boundaries, interface contracts, and data flow before writing implementation. In teamchat, produced a full v0.2 spec with technical decisions before code. In claude-narrator, defined a six-module pipeline with pluggable interfaces before implementation.

evidence.evidence_snippet: Produced detailed spec with module boundaries, tech decisions, and deferred features before any code

problem_solving_patterns.description: Decomposes every project into independent, composable modules with clear interfaces. Prefers pluggable abstractions over monolithic implementations.

evidence.evidence_snippet: Designed NarratorInterface supporting Claude API, local Ollama, and manual mode with same contract

problem_solving_patterns.description: Creates CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and spec documents as first-class artifacts. Documentation is a design tool, not an afterthought.

evidence.evidence_snippet: Wrote detailed CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md as shared context for agent team coordination

problem_solving_patterns.description: Regularly reflects on process and builds tools that observe their own creation. The blog-a-claude project documents its own construction; claude-candidate evaluates its own builder.

evidence.evidence_snippet: Designed tool where session logs from building it become input to it evaluating the builder

problem_solving_patterns.description: Explicitly defers features and manages complexity. Real Slack/Discord integration deferred to v2 in teamchat. Fine-tuning deferred in claude-narrator.

evidence.evidence_snippet: Explicitly deferred real Slack/Discord integration to v2, kept v1 focused on local JSON rendering

problem_solving_patterns.description: Adapts when approaches fail. Pivoted claude-pane-pulse to claude-code-pulse when Claude TUI title overwriting proved unsolvable. Diagnosed mode:plan bug during teamchat build and respawned agents.

evidence.evidence_snippet: Diagnosed ~20-minute loop caused by mode:plan bug, respawned agents with mode:auto to recover

counter_evidence.evidence_snippet: Took approximately 20 minutes to identify the mode:plan issue — not immediately diagnosed

problem_solving_patterns.description: Evaluates tools carefully, preferring open-source and modular options. Chose custom React over chat UI libraries for teamchat. Chose Bun over npm/yarn for speed.

evidence.evidence_snippet: Evaluated chat UI libraries, chose custom React+Tailwind for full control over rendering

working_style_summary: Architecture-first developer who designs module boundaries and interface contracts before writing code. Works primarily in Python and TypeScript, building CLI tools and developer-facing infrastructure. Thinks in systems — projects are modular, composable, and often recursive (tools that observe their own creation). Strong preference for open-source, honest documentation over polished curation, and free tooling over paid alternatives. Comfortable managing multiple concurrent agent sessions and complex terminal environments.

projects.description: TypeScript Obsidian plugin with Python companion script. Compiles browser history, shell history, and Claude Code session logs into daily Obsidian notes with AI summarization.
key_decisions: Privacy-first 4-tier escalation chain, all opt-in, local AI default
MIT license for Obsidian ecosystem compatibility
WASM SQLite for browser history reading in plugin context
challenges_overcome: Managing external contributor (slegarraga) code review and merge

key_decisions: Custom React + Tailwind over chat UI libraries for full rendering control
Bun for file watching with debounce
challenges_overcome: Broadcast message detection and idle notification suppression

communication_style: Precise and systems-oriented. Explains technical concepts through architecture metaphors and module boundaries rather than analogies. Asks clarifying questions about scope before starting. Writes documentation as a design tool.
extraction_notes: Sessions heavily weighted toward Python/TypeScript CLI tools and AI integration. Limited evidence of frontend design (beyond React components in teamchat), database administration, or cloud infrastructure. No evidence of team management or people leadership — all projects are individual contributor work. Session corpus does not capture pre-2025 career history.
